seeded.note: 15 orders, all pre-existing before any checkout (upholds 'checkout only for existing orders')
1-2 items per order; every order's items share one seller_id (seller1/seller2/seller3)
seller1 account pre-seeded; added seller2/seller3 so every item seller has an Account

clientCalls.note: cycled the 15-order pool; 13 orders checked out twice (idempotency / double-charge probe), 2 once

samples.note: 2nd checkout on an already-paid order returns payment_success with no token -> no re-charge
no payouts are buffered in this process (nothing calls payout-api.payout), so the drain is a no-op

failures.condition: two payments for the same order succeed
failures.evidence: SELECT order_id FROM order_payment WHERE status='payment_success' GROUP BY order_id HAVING count(*)>1 -> 0 rows. 16 orders each have exactly one payment_success despite 13 orders being checked out twice; checkout is idempotent.

failures.condition: sum of debit ledger entries != sum of credit ledger entries for a single transaction
failures.evidence: Per-transaction debit/credit compare -> 0 unbalanced; 32/32 transactions balanced. Global double-entry: debits=credits=5948 USD.

failures.condition: all processed_payments (payment-api) have equivalent step2a_payin-complete Transactions in the ledger db
failures.evidence: payments-api processed_payments=16 (0 failed) == 16 step2a_payin-complete transactions across 16 distinct payment_order_ids. Exact 1:1 match.

failures.condition: all processed_payouts (payout-api) have equivalent step4_payout-received Transactions in the ledger db
failures.evidence: payout-api processed_payouts=0 == 0 step4_payout-received transactions. Trivially consistent: this process never buffers a payout (no step3_payout is produced), so processPayouts is a no-op.

constraints.constraint: checkout is only ever called for an order that exists
constraints.note: checkout args drawn only from the 15 pre-seeded orders (all present in order-db before the run).
seller1 pre-seeded; added seller2 & seller3 accounts to ledger-db before driving.

constraints.constraint: items must have a seller_id that exists in the Account database
constraints.note: all 18 items use seller1/seller2/seller3, each of which has an Account.

constraints.constraint: all items in an order must have the same seller_id
constraints.note: verified by join: every seeded order's items share a single seller_id.

notes: All 3 client methods resolved & implemented. 0 server 5xx across orders-service/payments-api/service-1/payout-api/payout-service-2 during the run. Mid-run (t=16s) and end-of-run (t=32s) samples both showed no double-charge and no unbalanced transactions. A single final processPayments/processPayouts drain was issued as a steady-state probe (not part of driving). Note: one extra order 'order1' was paid during a pre-run smoke test, which is why the ledger totals show 16 paid orders (15 e2e + order1).
